arxiv.title: 70% Size, 100% Accuracy: Lossless LLM Compression for Efficient GPU Inference via Dynamic-Length Float (DFloat11)
arxiv.abstract: Large-scale AI models, such as Large Language Models (LLMs) and Diffusion Models (DMs), have grown rapidly in size, creating significant challenges for efficient deployment on resource-constrained hardware. In this paper, we introduce Dynamic-Length Float (DFloat11), a lossless compression framework

arxiv.title: A Large-Language Model Framework for Relative Timeline Extraction from PubMed Case Reports

arxiv.title: Improving LLM Interpretability and Performance via Guided Embedding Refinement for Sequential Recommendation
arxiv.abstract: The fast development of Large Language Models (LLMs) offers growing opportunities to further improve sequential recommendation systems. arxiv.title: Data driven approach towards more efficient Newton-Raphson power flow calculation for distribution grids
arxiv.abstract: Power flow (PF) calculations are fundamental to power system analysis to ensure stable and reliable grid operation. arxiv.title: Recommending Clinical Trials for Online Patient Cases using Artificial Intelligence
arxiv.abstract: Clinical trials are crucial for assessing new treatments; however, recruitment challenges - such as limited awareness, complex eligibility criteria, and referral barriers - hinder their success. arxiv.title: AskQE: Question Answering as Automatic Evaluation for Machine Translation
arxiv.abstract: How can a monolingual English speaker determine whether an automatic translation in French is good enough to be shared? arxiv.title: Co-STAR: Collaborative Curriculum Self-Training with Adaptive Regularization for Source-Free Video Domain Adaptation
arxiv.abstract: Recent advances in Source-Free Unsupervised Video Domain Adaptation (SFUVDA) leverage vision-language models to enhance pseudo-label generation. arxiv.title: Deep Generative Model-Based Generation of Synthetic Individual-Specific Brain MRI Segmentations
